```typescript
    token: Hex; // token address (bytes32 in spec)
    amount: bigint; // token amount in smallest unit
    recipient: Hex; // recipient address (bytes32 in spec)
    chainId: number; // chain ID where this transfer exists
}
```

#### FillInstruction {#fillinstruction}

ERC-7683 fill instruction for destination chain.

```typescript
interface FillInstruction {
    destinationChainId: number;
    destinationSettler: Hex;
    originData: Hex; // protocol-specific data passed to fill
}
```

#### OrderTrackingInfo {#ordertrackinginfo}

```typescript
interface OrderTrackingInfo<TMetadata = unknown> {
    status: OrderStatus;
    orderId: Hex;
    openTxHash: Hex;
    user: Address;
    originChainId: number;
    openDeadline: number;
    fillDeadline: number;
    maxSpent: TokenTransfer[];
    minReceived: TokenTransfer[];
    fillInstructions: FillInstruction[];
    fillEvent?: FillEvent<TMetadata>; // present when status is Finalized
    failureReason?: OrderFailureReason; // present when status is Failed
}
```

#### OrderTrackingUpdate {#ordertrackingupdate}

```typescript
interface OrderTrackingUpdate {
    status: OrderStatus;
    orderId?: Hex; // available after order is parsed
    openTxHash?: Hex; // undefined for escrow orders
    fillTxHash?: Hex; // available when completed
    timestamp: number; // unix timestamp in seconds
    message: string; // human-readable status message
    failureReason?: OrderFailureReason;
}
```

#### FillEvent {#fillevent}

```typescript
interface FillEvent<TMetadata = unknown> {
    fillTxHash: Hex;
    blockNumber?: bigint;
    timestamp: number;
    originChainId: number;
    orderId: Hex;
    relayer?: Address;
    recipient?: Address;
    metadata?: TMetadata;
}
```

#### OrderFailureReason {#orderfailurereason}

```typescript
const OrderFailureReason = {
    OriginTxReverted: "origin_tx_reverted",
    DeadlineExceeded: "deadline_exceeded",
    Unknown: "unknown",
} as const;

type OrderFailureReason = (typeof OrderFailureReason)[keyof typeof OrderFailureReason];
```

#### AssetDiscoveryOptions {#assetdiscoveryoptions}

```typescript
interface AssetDiscoveryOptions {
    chainIds?: number[]; // filter by chain ID(s)
}
```

#### DiscoveredAssets {#discoveredassets}

```typescript
interface DiscoveredAssets {
    /** Token addresses grouped by numeric chain ID */
    tokensByChain: Record<number, readonly string[]>;
    /** Token metadata nested by chain ID then lowercase address, with provider attribution */
    tokenMetadata: Record<number, Record<string, DiscoveredAssetInfo>>;
}

interface DiscoveredAssetInfo {
    address: string;
    symbol: string;
    decimals: number;
    providers: string[]; // which provider IDs reported this asset
}
```

#### RouteQuery {#routequery}

```typescript
interface RouteQuery {
    originChainId: number;
    originAsset: string; // plain 0x address
    destinationChainId: number;
    destinationAsset: string; // plain 0x address
}
```

### Provider Configuration {#provider-configuration}

#### Across {#provider-across}

| Field        | Type    | Required | Description                                   |
| ------------ | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `isTestnet`  | boolean | No       | Use testnet API (default: false)              |
| `apiUrl`     | string  | No       | Custom API endpoint URL (overrides isTestnet) |
| `providerId` | string  | No       | Custom provider identifier                    |

Payload validation:

| Operation                           | Validation                                                    |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Simple bridge (same token)          | Full validation (depositor, recipient, tokens, amount, chain) |
| Cross-chain swap (different tokens) | Coming soon                                                   |

#### Relay {#provider-relay}

| Field        | Type    | Required | Description                                   |
| ------------ | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `baseUrl`    | string  | No       | Custom API base URL (overrides isTestnet)     |
| `isTestnet`  | boolean | No       | Use testnet API (default: false)              |
| `providerId` | string  | No       | Custom provider identifier (default: "relay") |
| `apiKey`     | string  | No       | Relay API key for authentication              |

#### OIF {#provider-oif}

| Field             | Type     | Required | Description                                                                                                |
| ----------------- | -------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `solverId`        | string   | Yes      | Solver identifier                                                                                          |
| `url`             | string   | Yes      | Solver API endpoint URL                                                                                    |
| `headers`         | object   | No       | Custom HTTP headers                                                                                        |
| `adapterMetadata` | object   | No       | Additional metadata for the solver                                                                         |
| `providerId`      | string   | No       | Custom provider identifier                                                                                 |
| `supportedLocks`  | string[] | No       | Lock mechanisms to request (e.g. `["oif-escrow"]`, `["compact-resource-lock"]`). Default: `["oif-escrow"]` |
| `submissionModes` | string[] | No       | Execution modes: `["user-transaction"]`, `["gasless"]`, or both (default). Controls order types            |

Lock mechanism mapping:

| Lock Mechanism          | OIF Order Types                |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `oif-escrow`            | `oif-escrow-v0`, `oif-3009-v0` |
| `compact-resource-lock` | `oif-resource-lock-v0`         |

Supported order types:

-   `oif-escrow-v0` — Permit2-based escrow (gasless)
-   `oif-3009-v0` — EIP-3009 transfer with authorization (gasless)
-   `oif-resource-lock-v0` — Compact resource locking (gasless)
-   `oif-user-open-v0` — User executes transaction directly

Payload validation:

| Order Type             | Validation                                |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `oif-escrow-v0`        | token, amount, deadline                   |
| `oif-resource-lock-v0` | token, amount, sponsor, expiration        |
| `oif-3009-v0`          | from, value, token address, expiration    |
| `oif-user-open-v0`     | allowances (token, user, spender, amount) |

## Error Reference {#error-reference}

All error classes are exported from `@wonderland/interop-cross-chain`. They extend the native `Error` class unless noted.

### Provider Errors {#provider-errors}

#### `ProviderNotFound` {#error-providernotfound}

Thrown when a requested provider ID is not registered in the aggregator.

```typescript
class ProviderNotFound extends Error {
    constructor(providerName: string)
}
```

Example message: `Provider across not found`

---

#### `ProviderGetQuoteFailure` {#error-providergetquotefailure}

Thrown when a provider fails to fetch quotes due to an internal error or unexpected API response. Extends `BaseError`.

```typescript
class ProviderGetQuoteFailure extends BaseError {
    constructor(message: string, cause?: string, stack?: string)
}
```

Example message: `across: failed to parse quote response`

---

#### `ProviderExecuteNotImplemented` {#error-providerexecutenotimplemented}

Thrown when `buildQuote` or `submitOrder` is called on a provider that does not implement that method. The error message is the provider ID string.

```typescript
class ProviderExecuteNotImplemented extends Error {
    constructor(providerId: string)
}
```

Example message: `relay` (the provider ID is used as the message directly)

---

#### `ProviderExecuteFailure` {#error-providerexecutefailure}

Thrown when order execution fails for a provider-specific reason. Extends `BaseError`.

```typescript
class ProviderExecuteFailure extends BaseError {
    constructor(message: string, cause?: string, stack?: string)
}
```

---

#### `ProviderGetStatusFailure` {#error-providergetstatusfailure}

Thrown when polling for order status fails. Extends `BaseError`.

```typescript
class ProviderGetStatusFailure extends BaseError {
    constructor(message: string, cause?: string, stack?: string)
}
```

---

#### `ProviderConfigFailure` {#error-providerconfigfailure}

Thrown when a provider receives an invalid or incomplete configuration. Extends `BaseError`.

```typescript
class ProviderConfigFailure extends BaseError {
    constructor(message: string, cause?: string, stack?: string)
}
```

---

#### `ProviderTimeout` {#error-providertimeout}

Thrown (or yielded) when a provider does not respond within the configured `timeoutMs`.

```typescript
class ProviderTimeout extends Error {
    constructor(message: string)
}
```

Example message: `Timeout after 15000ms`

---

#### `UnsupportedProtocol` {#error-unsupportedprotocol}

Thrown by `createCrossChainProvider` when the given protocol name is not registered.

```typescript
class UnsupportedProtocol extends Error {
    constructor(protocolName: string)
}
```

Example message: `Unsupported protocol: myProtocol`

### Asset Errors {#asset-errors}

#### `UnsupportedAsset` {#error-unsupportedasset}

Thrown when a provider does not support the requested input/output asset route.

```typescript
class UnsupportedAsset extends Error {
    constructor(
        providerId: string,
        input: { assetAddress: string; chainId: number },
        output: { assetAddress: string; chainId: number },
    )
}
```

Example message: `Provider "across" does not support the route 0xA0b8...eB48 (chain 1) -> 0xaf88...831 (chain 42161)`

---

#### `DifferentAssetNotAllowed` {#error-differentassetnotallowed}

Thrown when `buildQuote` is called with different input and output token addresses. Cross-token swaps must go through `getQuotes()`.

```typescript
class DifferentAssetNotAllowed extends Error {
    constructor()
}
```

Example message: `buildQuote only supports same-asset transfers. Cross-token swaps are supported through getQuotes(), where solvers handle pricing and fee calculation.`

---

#### `AssetDiscoveryFailure` {#error-assetdiscoveryfailure}

Thrown when asset discovery fails for a provider.

```typescript
class AssetDiscoveryFailure extends Error {
    public readonly details?: string;
    public readonly originalStack?: string;
    constructor(message: string, details?: string, originalStack?: string)
}
```

### Validation Errors {#validation-errors}

#### `ZeroAmount` {#error-zeroamount}

Thrown when `buildQuote` receives a zero input or output amount.

```typescript
class ZeroAmount extends Error {
    constructor(field: "input" | "output")
}
```

Example message: `input.amount must be greater than zero`

---

#### `InsufficientFee` {#error-insufficientfee}

Thrown when `buildQuote` detects that `output.amount >= input.amount`, leaving no fee margin.

```typescript
class InsufficientFee extends Error {
    constructor(inputAmount: string, outputAmount: string)
}
```

Example message: `output.amount (1000000) must be less than input.amount (1000000) to allow for a fee margin`

---

#### `InvalidDeadline` {#error-invaliddeadline}

Thrown when `buildQuote` receives a `fillDeadline` that is in the past or too close to the current time.

```typescript
class InvalidDeadline extends Error {
    public readonly deadline: number;
    public readonly nowSeconds: number;
    constructor(
        deadline: number,
        nowSeconds: number,
        reason: "past" | "too-soon",
        minBuffer?: number,
    )
}
```

Example messages:
- `fillDeadline (1700000000) is in the past (now: 1712000000)`
- `fillDeadline is too soon: 30s from now, minimum is 60s`

---

#### `SameChainIntentNotAllowed` {#error-samechainintentnotallowed}

Thrown when `buildQuote` is called with the same chain ID for input and output.

```typescript
class SameChainIntentNotAllowed extends Error {
    constructor()
}
```

Example message: `buildQuote does not support same-chain intents. Use a direct transfer instead.`

---

#### `UnsupportedChainId` {#error-unsupportedchainid}

Thrown when an operation is requested for a chain ID the SDK does not recognise.

```typescript
class UnsupportedChainId extends Error {
    constructor(chainId: number)
}
```

Example message: `Non supported chain id: 9999`

---

#### `UnsupportedAddress` {#error-unsupportedaddress}

Thrown when an address string does not match the expected format.

```typescript
class UnsupportedAddress extends Error {
    constructor(address: string)
}
```

Example message: `Unsupported address: not-an-address`

---

#### `UnsupportedAction` {#error-unsupportedaction}

Thrown when a requested action type is not supported by the current provider or context.

```typescript
class UnsupportedAction extends Error {
    constructor(action: string)
}
```

Example message: `Non-supported action: swap`

### Network / RPC Errors {#network-errors}

#### `APIRequestFailure` {#error-apirequestfailure}

Thrown when an HTTP API call returns a non-2xx, non-404 status. Exposes the raw HTTP status code and response body.

```typescript
class APIRequestFailure extends Error {
    public readonly status: number;
    public readonly body: string;
    constructor(protocol: string, status: number, body: string)
}
```

Example message: `across API request failed with status 429: rate limit exceeded`

---

#### `GetTokenAllowanceIssue` {#error-gettokenallowanceissue}

Thrown when the SDK fails to read a token allowance from the chain (e.g., RPC error).

```typescript
class GetTokenAllowanceIssue extends Error {
    constructor(message: string)
}
```

---

#### `GetTokenDecimalsIssue` {#error-gettokendecimals-issue}

Thrown when the SDK fails to read token decimals from the chain (e.g., RPC error or non-ERC20 contract).

```typescript
class GetTokenDecimalsIssue extends Error {
    constructor(message: string)
}
```

### Tracking Errors {#tracking-errors}

#### `OpenedIntentNotFoundError` {#error-openedintentnotfound}

Thrown when no ERC-7683 Open event is found in the given transaction receipt.

```typescript
class OpenedIntentNotFoundError extends Error {
    constructor(txHash: Hex, protocol: string)
}
```

Example message: `across opened intent event not found in transaction 0xabc...`

---

#### `OIFOpenEventNotFoundError` {#error-oifopeneventnotfound}

Thrown when an OIF standard Open event is missing from a transaction.

```typescript
class OIFOpenEventNotFoundError extends Error {
    constructor(txHash: Hex)
}
```

Example message: `OIF Open event not found in transaction 0xabc...`

---

#### `InvalidOpenEventError` {#error-invalidopenevent}

Thrown when an Open event is found but its data cannot be parsed.

```typescript
class InvalidOpenEventError extends Error {
    constructor(message: string)
}
```

Example message: `Invalid Open event: missing orderId field`

## References {#references}

-   [Cross-chain Protocol Standards](https://github.com/ethereum/ercs/blob/master/ERCS/erc-5164.md)
-   [EIP-7683: Cross Chain Intents](https://www.erc7683.org/)
-   [Open Intents Framework](https://github.com/openintentsframework)


---
